'''Thota''' were a savage [[Species/Legends|species]] of predatory creature native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Varl/Legends|Varl]]. They were considered the primary predators of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[t'landa Til]] species who also dwelt on their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. The predators were capable of producing a relatively high pitch, shrill wail, the sound of which caused t'landa Till to panic and flee mindlessly.<ref name="RD" /> The thota's homeworld had its atmosphere destroyed in a cataclysmic event which left the planet barren and poisoned, killing most of the creatures on it.<ref name="EA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]] the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Sniquux]] used a recording of a thota's wail to panic a group of six t'landa Til [[Priest/Legends|priests]] in [[Colony Nine]], a colony [[City/Legends|city]] on the planet [[Ylesia/Legends|Ylesia]]. The priests attempted to locate the source of the sound then fled from the noise, causing them to run into a number of [[monofilament wire]]s that the Rodian had set up, which [[Death/Legends|killed]] them. By this year the thota were considered ancient.<ref name="RD" />

==Behind the scenes==
The thota were first mentioned in the novel ''[[Rebel Dawn]]'' released in [[1998]] which was the second book in [[The Han Solo Trilogy (hardcover)|''The Han Solo Trilogy'']] written by [[A. C. Crispin]]. It has not been made clear in any source if the thota survived the cataclysm on Varl.
